T1566.name: Antivirus/Antimalware
T1566.description: Deploy email gateway filtering, sandboxing of attachments, and user security awareness training to detect and block phishing attempts.

T1566.001.name: Antivirus/Antimalware + Email Filtering
T1566.001.description: Block executable attachments at the email gateway. Enable macro-disabled policies in Office. Sandbox suspicious attachments before delivery.

T1566.002.name: Restrict Web-Based Content
T1566.002.description: Implement URL filtering and web proxies to block known malicious domains. Train users to verify links before clicking.

T1059.name: Disable or Remove Feature or Program
T1059.description: Restrict PowerShell execution policies, disable Windows Script Host, apply application whitelisting (AppLocker/WDAC) to prevent unauthorized script execution.

T1059.001.name: PowerShell Restriction
T1059.001.description: Enable PowerShell Constrained Language Mode, enforce script signing, log all PowerShell activity via ScriptBlock logging and Module logging.

T1053.name: Operating System Configuration
T1053.description: Restrict scheduled task creation to privileged users. Monitor Task Scheduler for newly created or modified tasks. Audit schtasks.exe usage.

T1078.name: Multi-factor Authentication
T1078.description: Enforce MFA for all remote access and privileged accounts. Monitor for anomalous login patterns, impossible travel, and credential stuffing.

T1190.name: Update Software
T1190.description: Apply security patches promptly, especially for internet-facing applications. Deploy WAF rules. Conduct regular vulnerability scanning and penetration testing.

T1133.name: Limit Access to Resource Over Network
T1133.description: Restrict RDP and VPN access to authorized users. Use network-level authentication (NLA). Implement IP whitelisting and VPN with MFA.

T1021.name: Multi-factor Authentication
T1021.description: Enforce MFA for remote services (SMB, RDP, WinRM). Restrict lateral movement paths using network segmentation and privileged access workstations.

T1486.name: Data Backup
T1486.description: Maintain regular offline backups. Test restoration procedures. Implement volume shadow copy protection and endpoint detection for encryption behavior.

T1055.name: Behavior Prevention on Endpoint
T1055.description: Deploy EDR with memory protection capabilities. Enable Windows Credential Guard. Monitor for suspicious process injection APIs (WriteProcessMemory, NtMapViewOfSection).

T1003.name: Credential Access Protection
T1003.description: Enable Windows Credential Guard and LSASS protection (RunAsPPL). Restrict debug privileges. Monitor for LSASS memory access attempts.

T1003.001.name: LSASS Memory Protection
T1003.001.description: Enable Credential Guard and configure LSASS as Protected Process Light. Monitor for tools like Mimikatz, ProcDump accessing LSASS memory.

T1047.name: Privileged Account Management
T1047.description: Restrict WMI access to authorized administrators. Monitor WMI event subscriptions and remote WMI activity. Disable DCOM if not required.

T1548.name: Audit
T1548.description: Enable enhanced auditing for privilege escalation events. Monitor for UAC bypass techniques, sudo misconfigurations, and SUID/SGID abuse.

T1562.name: Restrict File and Directory Permissions
T1562.description: Protect security tool configurations and log files with strict ACLs. Monitor for tampering with AV/EDR services, Event Log, and audit policies.

T1071.name: Network Intrusion Prevention
T1071.description: Deploy network IDS/IPS to detect C2 traffic patterns. Monitor for unusual DNS queries, beaconing behavior, and encrypted traffic to suspicious destinations.

T1048.name: Network Intrusion Prevention
T1048.description: Monitor for unusual outbound data transfers. Deploy DLP solutions. Restrict outbound connections to approved services. Inspect encrypted traffic at proxy.

T1105.name: Network Intrusion Prevention
T1105.description: Block known malicious file transfer protocols and domains. Monitor for downloads from uncommon sources. Deploy application whitelisting.

T1547.name: Execution Prevention
T1547.description: Monitor registry Run keys, startup folders, and boot configuration for unauthorized modifications. Use application whitelisting to prevent execution from startup locations.

T1112.name: Restrict Registry Permissions
T1112.description: Restrict write access to critical registry keys. Enable registry auditing. Monitor for modifications to Run/RunOnce keys, services, and security settings.

T1027.name: Antivirus/Antimalware
T1027.description: Deploy behavioral analysis and sandboxing solutions. Monitor for encoded/compressed executables, unusual file types, and packed binaries.

T1036.name: Code Signing
T1036.description: Enforce code signing requirements. Monitor for executables masquerading as legitimate system binaries. Verify digital signatures of running processes.

T1070.name: Remote Data Storage
T1070.description: Forward logs to a centralized SIEM in real-time. Protect log integrity with write-once storage. Monitor for Event Log clearing and timestomping activity.

T1530.name: Multi-factor Authentication + Cloud ACLs
T1530.description: Enforce strict IAM policies on cloud storage. Enable access logging and alerting. Implement least-privilege bucket policies and encryption at rest.

T1195.name: Update Software
T1195.description: Verify software integrity via hash comparison. Use trusted software repositories. Monitor for unexpected changes in build pipelines and update mechanisms.

T1098.name: Multi-factor Authentication
T1098.description: Monitor for unexpected account modifications, especially addition of credentials, group membership changes, and permission escalations in Active Directory.

T1082.name: Detection-Focused Mitigation
T1082.description: This technique is difficult to prevent. Focus on detection: monitor for enumeration commands (systeminfo, hostname, whoami) executed in rapid succession.

T1018.name: Network Intrusion Prevention
T1018.description: Monitor for network scanning activity (port scans, ping sweeps). Restrict internal network visibility with segmentation. Alert on unusual SMB/LDAP enumeration.
